formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Phosphoglucomutase is the enzyme commonly found in both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis due to its ability to reversibly convert glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate. This dual function makes it critical at the metabolic crossroads where carbohydrate storage and mobilization intersect. 

\boxed{\text{phosphoglucomutase}} Final Answer: \boxed{D}

The correct enzyme common to both glycogenesis and glycogenolysis is **phosphoglucomutase**, as it catalyzes the reversible conversion of glucose-1-phosphate to glucose-6-phosphate in both pathways. It serves as a key regulatory link enabling metabolism to switch efficiently between energy storage and release.  

\boxed{D}. Answer choice: D
